title: Conditional coloring should allow you to compare the values of two columns
excerpt: I’d like to be able to use conditional coloring when Column A not equal to Column B…or the opposite when Column A is equal to Column B but this option does not exist. Please add the ability to compare two column values.

posts.author: kelliem (Kellie M)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to be able to use conditional coloring when Column A not equal to Column B…or the opposite when Column A is equal to Column B but this option does not exist. Please add the ability to compare two column values.

posts.author: User129438 (John S)
posts.cookedHtml: Same, this is the main obvious use case for this currently useless feature

posts.author: ChristineWLF (Christine)
posts.cookedHtml: Still interested in this feature 2 years later

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Have you tried to utilize a formula column and do the value comparison there.
Then set the conditional coloring on the formula column.
If formula column is “not equal” set row/cell color.
